summarization.a: This two-panel figure shows Mass Change Per Cycle (MCPC) (ng/(cm²·cycle)) data for the Atomic Layer Etching (ALE) of Al₂O₃, MCPC varies the NF₃ plasma time (with fixed 3s TMA)
summarization.b: Mass Change Per Cycle (MCPC) (ng/(cm²·cycle)) varies the TMA pulse time (with fixed 4s NF₃). Both plots show the mass loss saturating, proving each half-reaction is self-limiting.

data_extraction.a:
| NF₃ Remote Plasma Time (s) | MCPC (ng/(cm²·cycle)) | Symbol    |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 0 |NF₃ remote plasma (square) |
| 1.5 | -25 | NF₃ remote plasma (square) |
| 2.0 | -60 | NF₃ remote plasma (square) |
| 3.0 | -70 | NF₃ remote plasma (square) |
| 4.0 | -60 | NF₃ remote plasma (square) |
| 4.0 | -2  | NF₃ gas (circle) |

a.question: If the fixed TMA time in this chart were reduced to 1 second (undersaturated), how would the saturation curve change? Would you still achieve a stable, self-limiting etch process?
a.answer: The curve would still rise but plateau at a lower maximum mass loss. With an undersaturated TMA dose, not all the fluorinated layer is removed each cycle. The process would not be fully self-limiting or stable, as the etch depth would depend on the incomplete removal step, leading to poor control and possible residue accumulation.

a.question: Explain the physical significance of the 'grey zone' (0–1.2 s) in graph. Why is the mass loss negligible in this region despite the presence of plasma?
a.answer: The grey region represents an incubation or threshold period. During these short exposures, the incoming fluorine radical flux is too low to fully fluorinate the Al₂O₃ surface to the depth needed for the subsequent ligand-exchange reaction with TMA. Because a sufficient coverage of surface fluoride (AlFₓ) species has not yet formed, TMA cannot remove material, resulting in nearly zero MCPC.

a.question: Calculate the etch rate in Å per cycle assuming the density of Al₂O₃ is approximately 3.0 g/cm³ and the saturated mass loss is −60 ng/(cm²·cycle). Based on this etch rate, determine how many cycles would be required to remove a 5 nm thick Al₂O₃ film.

b.question: List the four main sequential steps for one complete ALE cycle used to generate the saturation point (e.g., at 3s TMA time).
b.answer:
